title: salix aurita var. uliginosa
intro: Salix aurita var. uliginosa, also known as marsh willow, is a variety of the willow tree (Salix aurita) that is native to Northern Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Salix aurita var. uliginosa lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The health benefits of using Salix aurita var. uliginosa include reducing fever, relieving headaches and joint pain, and acting as an anti-inflammatory agent. Salix aurita var. uliginosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
